category_title: Year 4 student with ADHD symptoms and Literacy difficulties
student_description: Yiannis is a highly active student who struggles significantly with sustained attention and physical restlessness, finding independent tasks like writing extremely daunting. He uniquely requires tasks to be micro-chunked to prevent refusal, thrives on explicit praise and reminders of past success, and needs subtle, non-verbal redirection rather than direct verbal reprimands.

criteria.rationale: Presenting an entire task at once causes Yiannis to feel overwhelmed and refuse to work.
criteria.check: The tutor breaks the current academic task into a single, small, manageable step rather than giving multi-step or open-ended instructions.

criteria.rationale: Yiannis craves praise and needs reminders of his capabilities to overcome his fear of failure, especially on off days.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly includes positive reinforcement, praise, or a reminder of a past success when giving an instruction or encouraging him to continue.

criteria.rationale: Direct verbal reprimands or repeatedly calling his name are unappreciated and ineffective for managing his physical restlessness.
criteria.check: The tutor addresses distraction or restlessness without using direct reprimands or repeatedly calling his name, instead using subtle redirection or offering a sanctioned short break.
